items.title: Security fears as WA prepares to roll out digital drivers licences

children.content: A cybersecurity expert warns people's privacy and security could be at risk once the state government rolls out its new digital drivers licence initiative.

items.title: Two young girls die as WA records another dire weekend on roads

children.content: Two young girls, aged three and four, die more than a week after a car crash in Viveash, with the news emerging after another two fatalities on WA roads over the Anzac Day long weekend.

image.altText: A section of road with some trees alongside.

items.title: 'We live up here to escape': Residents unhappy with sand dredging proposal

children.content: A Dutch dredging company acknowledges concerns from some community members in northern Western Australia that public meetings should have been held earlier.

image.altText: A man sits next to some reeds near a river

items.title: Indigenous veteran booed at Anzac Day service 'mortified' by heckling

children.content: The Whadjuk Noongar elder who was booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth's Anzac Day dawn service says she had a right to speak at the event and will not back down.

image.altText: An older woman, an Aboriginal elder, sits at an outdoor event wearing a dark jacket with military medals pinned to it.

items.title: Elder who was booed reacts to RSL's planned Welcome to Country review

children.content: RSL Australia will review its Welcome to Country guidelines after Indigenous elders were booed during Anzac Day services across the country.

image.altText: People gathering for an Anzac Day service in Sydney's Martin Place

items.title: Supreme Court Justice says new act 'impossible' to fully implement

children.content: WA legal experts say the Criminal Law Mental Impairment Act isn't working as intended, citing a case in Broome where a man was found not criminally responsible but still jailed.

A photograph of a title page of the Mentally Impaired Accused Act

items.title: Former Republican congressman nominated as US ambassador to Australia

children.content: David Brat, who unseated a Democratic house majority leader, has been named as the Trump administration's pick for the Canberra posting. Follow live.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit speaks to an audience.

items.title: Taylor's $800m pitch to double fuel reserves to come at a cost to drivers

children.content: Australia would have 60 days' worth of fuel supplies in reserve under an $800 million Coalition proposal that would cost drivers one cent per litre.

image.altText: Angus Taylor speaking to media standing outside a building.

items.title: AI architects become their own victims

children.content: Tim Cook helped usher in a communications revolution and his departure from Apple marks a turning point for the company. It also serves as a reminder of how AI is changing the tech landscape.

articles.title: This basket isn't real, but it might affect your mortgage repayments

children.content: There are some key moments coming up for the Australian economy and they could make a big difference to your bank account. Emily Stewart — aka Sensible Emily — is here to help you understand what to look out for.

articles.title: Wall Street hits records as Australia awaits inflation data

children.content: Two of the key US indices are at record highs despite uncertainty about the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Indigenous players defend Lyon after comment at training led to 'emotional' meeting

children.content: Several indigenous players who have played under Saints coach Ross Lyon come to his defence after comments he made during a training session led to a meeting where Lyon offered to stand down.

image.altText: ross1

items.title: They were the first victims of the Port Arthur massacre. Their family has never forgotten

children.content: Seascape guesthouse owners Sally and David Martin were the first people murdered at Port Arthur in 1996. On the 30th anniversary of the tragedy, their granddaughter tells of how the family has refused to be defined by it.

image.altText: A man and woman pose for a photo in front of a waterfall.

items.title: Aussie wins first World Surf League title at Margaret River Pro

children.content: Australian surfer George Pittar defeated a quartet of Brazilian superstars — including three-time world champion Gabriel Medina in the final — to claim a maiden championship tour win at the Margaret River Pro.

gallery.altText: A surfer wearing blue holds an Australian flag over his head with beach behind him

thumbnail.altText: A surfer wearing blue holds an Australian flag over his head with beach behind him

items.title: 'Broken' PBS system behind failure to list weight loss medication: peak body

children.content: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

image.altText: A bottle of injectable GLP-1 medication and three injectable pens

items.title: The best ways to prevent common credit and debit card fraud

children.content: Experts reveal the most common ways fraudsters steal card details and how to keep your accounts safer.

items.title: The moment Andy Lee feared exploits with Hamish Blake had gone too far

children.content: Andy Lee opens up on his 23-year working relationship with his "fast and loose" comedy partner Hamish Blake, and the stunt that left him fearing for his mate's life.

items.title: SA Police search waterways for knives linked to alleged murder by Perth man

children.content: SA Police will search waterways in Adelaide's south today to find three knives they believe are connected to the alleged murder of 24-year-old Caleb Leriano.

image.altText: A young man with glasse smiles at the camera in a bright room

items.title: One industry rules the roost in WA and neither side of politics can ignore it

children.content: Last week marked the first time the federal Liberal leader visited WA since he took the position, and during his trip west, both Angus Taylor and Premier Roger Cook were navigating the state's hot-button issues.

items.title: Penny Wong confronts the limits of Australia's China strategy

children.content: When Foreign Minister Penny Wong travels to Japan, China and South Korea this week, she will move through a region where relationships point in different directions at the same time.

items.title: Mother's anguish after daughter dies in prison cell

children.content: Deaths in custody are on the rise and family members and advocates for a woman who died in a Perth prison say more needs to be done to keep inmates safe.

items.title: Bupa patients lose out on choice and risk out-of-pockets under No Gap scheme, experts warn

children.content: Leaked contracts obtained by the ABC reveal Bupa's deals with private hospitals impose stifling conditions on operators that are too small to push back.

items.title: Academic says she watches everything she eats because of overseas threats

children.content: Iranian Australian journalist and academic Saba Vasefi has spent years documenting human rights abuses in her homeland. The regime she's criticised has not forgotten her name.

items.title: Fremantle Prison gains 'unique' documents about Catalpa captain

children.content: Captain George Anthony's family share historic documents detailing the life of the principled Quaker who was branded a pirate for his role in the Catalpa rescue.

items.title: Dingo destroyed after week of attacks in WA's north-west

children.content: A male dingo has been "humanely destroyed" after multiple attacks within days of each other at Western Australia's famous Karijini National Park.
